The National Institute of Pharmaceutical Education and Research (NIPER) announced in Madurai has not yet been established. Actions will be taken to start this educational institution immediately. 11. An air ambulance scheme will be introduced to easily access high medical care centres in various states and cities worldwide. This service will be provided by the government. 12. The medical infrastructure in underprivileged areas will be improved in collaboration with private medical institutions known for their excellent service in India's medical infrastructure. 13. Financial incentives, housing benefits and opportunities for professional development in medicine will be provided to doctors willing to work in rural and backward areas. Constituency Delimitation: The successful implementation of the Union Government's family planning program by the Tamil Nadu Government led to a decrease in population, resulting in the reduction of parliamentary constituencies from 41 to 39 during the constituency delimitation in 1971.

Based on the amended law in 2002, the Union Government decided to maintain the number of constituencies at their previous level due to the insistence of the states, and the number of constituencies continues on this basis. The DMK assures that this status will continue during the upcoming constituency delimitation as well. Agriculture: 1. Actions will be taken through the National Agricultural Cooperative to purchase coconuts throughout the year at a base support price. 2. Agriculture Development Councils including agricultural representatives at the national, state, and district levels will be established to determine the Minimum Support Price (MSP) for bananas, turmeric, tapioca, pulses, chillies, millets, tea, oilseeds, horticultural products, etc. The government will procure an adequate amount of minor grains and sell them through fair price shops. 3. The Godavari-Kaveri interlinking project will be expedited to enhance Tamil Nadu's agriculture. 4. A project to link the rivers Pamba, Achankovil, and Vaippar, in consultation with the Kerala state government, will be implemented to aid irrigation. 5. GST on agricultural inputs such as pesticides and fertilizers will be reduced to benefit farmers. 6. The recommendations of the M.S.

Swaminathan committee will be adopted, ensuring that agricultural products' MSP is set at total production cost + 50%. 7. The production subsidy limit for paddy procured from a farmer for up to 2 hectares will be increased from the current limit to 10 tons. 8. Financial assistance under the PM-KISAN scheme will be increased to Rs. 12,000. 9. Actions will be taken to formulate climate-resilient agricultural policies to protect farmers, ensure food security, and mitigate the impact of seasonal changes. 10. Cold storage facilities will be established at the district level to protect the agricultural produce cultivated by farmers. 11. Measures will be taken to ensure the availability of quality chemical and organic fertilizers at fair prices. 12. Modern agricultural technologies and practices will be implemented. 13. Necessary agricultural machinery will be made available to farmers across the country either through ownership or government departments. 14. Efforts to construct the Mekedatu Dam will be stopped: Measures will be taken by the DMK through firm legal actions to protect the welfare of farmers in the delta region and to ensure water supply to various parts of Tamil Nadu, countering the Supreme Court's decision against the construction of the Mekedatu Dam.

Welfare of Armed Forces Personnel: 1. The Agnipath scheme will be withdrawn. 2. Permanent recruitment service for Indian Armed Forces personnel will be reintroduced. 3. The pension and other financial benefits for Indian Armed Forces personnel will be increased in recognition of their contribution to national security. Chennai: 1. A third railway terminal will be constructed in Chennai for the convenience of the public. 2. The long-delayed railway project between Velachery and St. Thomas Mount will be completed swiftly. 3. A subway pathway will be constructed from Villivakkam railway station southward for the benefit of the residents of Rajaji Nagar in the Kolathur Assembly constituency. 4. The Metro Rail project from Chennai Koyambedu Metro Station to Ambattur Industrial Estate will be expedited. 5. The Metro Rail route from Wimco Nagar Metro Station to Ennore will be extended. 6. The long-standing demand of the people of Thiruvottiyur for upgrading the Thiruvottiyur Wimco Nagar railway station under the Amrit Bharat scheme will be fulfilled, ensuring all trains from the north stop at Wimco Nagar. 7. An ESI hospital will be established in the Manali area to cater to diseases caused by industrial pollution affecting the local population. Thiruvallur: 1.

A high-level bridge will be constructed over NH5 near Pullian Village, Sengundram Town Panchayat in Puzhal Union, Ponneri Taluk of Thiruvallur District to facilitate road crossing. 2. A new bridge and road will be constructed between Pazhaverkadu and Kadappakkam. Kanchipuram: 1. A pedestrian bridge will be constructed to alleviate the difficulty of covering approximately a kilometre around the traffic congestion in the Poonamallee Highway-TP Chathiram area. 2. The project to convert the national highway from Thiruporur to Karapettai (NH 48 from km 37.000 to km 71.015) into a six-lane road will be completed swiftly. 3. The railway path from Chennai Central Railway Station and Chennai Egmore Railway Station will be converted into a double track to address the platform shortage, creating an alternative route from Arakkonam to Kanchipuram towards Chengalpattu. 4. The cargo transportation at Kanchipuram East Railway Station and the operation as Asia's largest automobile cargo hub will be further enhanced. 5. Additional electric train services will be emphasized to cater to the increasing travel needs of the people of Kanchipuram and its surrounding areas. Chengalpattu: 1. Integrated vaccine production facilities in Chengalpattu will be upgraded and production started. Vellore: 1.

A factory producing mango juice using the mangoes grown in hundreds of acres in the Paradarami area will be established. 2. The completed but yet-to-be-opened Vellore airport will be inaugurated soon. Ranipet: 1. A government arts or technical college will be established in the Arcot area. 2. A cooperative bank will be established in Vilapakkam Town. 3. A railway station will be set up in Sendhamangalam. 4. A community water supply scheme will be implemented in the areas under the Nemmeli Union. 5. The railway line construction from Nagari to Tindivanam will be completed swiftly. Tirupattur: 1. The Tirupati-Coimbatore Chennai Intercity Express will be made to stop at the Tirupattur railway station. 2. A high-level bridge will be constructed at the Vaniyambadi New Town LC81 railway gate. 3. Out of the two passenger trains departing from Erode to Salem Junction and proceeding to Jolarpettai at different times, one will be extended from Jolarpettai to Tirupati, and the other from Jolarpettai to Chennai Central. Krishnagiri: 1. A new airport will be established in Hosur, an emerging industrial city. 2. A flyover from Jujuvadi to SIPCOT 2, serving as an entry point to Tamil Nadu, will be constructed. 3.

A railway station will be set up in Krishnagiri. 4. Rail service from Jolarpettai to Hosur via Krishnagiri will be initiated. 5. The Hogenakkal intergrated drinking water project will be expanded. 6. Rail service from Hosur to Krishnagiri to Chennai will be started. 7. A subsidy-included 50-acre land in SIPCOT will be allocated for the development of the powerloom industry. 8. Efforts to start a Metro Rail service from Hosur to Bangalore will be undertaken. 9. A railway overbridge on the Hosur-Thali road will be constructed. Dharmapuri: A new broad-gauge rail line connecting Morappur and Dharmapuri railway stations will be constructed. Tiruvannamalai: 1. The long-pending rail line project between Tindivanam and Arani, via Nagari, will be expedited. 2. An export centre will be established for rice and paddy traders. 3. The daily train service that previously operated from Tiruvannamalai to Tirukoilur to Mambalappattu to Villupuram and onwards to Chennai, which has been suspended for ten years, will be reinstated for the convenience of the public. 4. A new toll gate that could potentially hinder urban development in Tiruvannamalai, a city rooted in spirituality and agriculture, will be cancelled. Viluppuram: 1.

In the new rail line project from Tindivanam to Nagari, a connecting path from Arani station to Onnupuram, as well as from Kanchipuram to Cheyyar, will be included. 2. A two-way rail line will be built between Viluppuram and Katpadi. 3. A new railway overbridge will be constructed on the railway road from Ulundurpet to Viluppuram NH45, leading to Padhur village, to prevent rainwater from stagnating. 4. A demand for at least one fast train to stop at the urban area railway station under the Ulundurpet constituency will be fulfilled. 5. The Chennai-Pondicherry passenger train will be arranged to stop at Vikravandi railway station. 6. The Tejas Express will be scheduled to stop in Viluppuram. 7. The passenger trains number 22671/22672 and 56703/56704 from Viluppuram to Trichy, currently operating on an alternative route only up to Virudhachalam, will be extended to operate up to Viluppuram. Kallakurichi: The railway underpass on Thiruvennainallur Road, Alanguppam, will be converted into an overbridge to prevent rainwater accumulation during the monsoon. Salem: 1. Salem Airport will be expanded and considered for development into an international airport. 2. A new industrial park will be set up in Salem over 1000 acres. 3.

Salem district is known for the production of textiles, sago, tapioca, coir, anklets, agriculture, etc. Hence, an integrated development centre will be established there. Namakkal: 1. A railway overbridge connecting Pasur in Erode district with Cholaramani in Namakkal district will be constructed. 2. A special egg export centre will be established in Namakkal.

Erode: 1. A new broad-gauge rail line project from Erode railway station to Kangayam, Dharapuram, and Palani railway stations will be completed. 2. A cancer research centre by the Union government will be established in the Erode parliamentary constituency. Tirupur: Proposals will be made to provide fee concessions to transport essential commodities like maize and soy through Indian Railways cargo trains from various parts of India to Tamil Nadu to reduce the cost of these essential grain supplies. The Nilgiris: 1. The minimum price for green tea leaves for small tea farmers in the Nilgiris district will be set at Rs 35 per kilo. 2. Tourist spots and access roads in the Nilgiris district will be developed through the Indian Tourism Development Corporation. Coimbatore: 1. Night train services from Coimbatore to southern districts for Tamil Nadu traders will be introduced. 2. Pollachi's tender coconut and coconut will receive GI tagging, and a COLD STORAGE facility will be established in Pollachi. 3. Pollachi railway station will be renovated. 4. The Cheran Express service will be extended to Pollachi. 5. Coimbatore Metro Rail Project service will be extended to Tirupur. 6. A broad gauge railway project from Mettupalayam-SathyamangalamGobichettipalayam-Erode will be completed.

Perambalur: A new broad-gauge railway line will be constructed from Attur railway station to Perambalur and Ariyalur railway station. Ariyalur: 1. A new rail line from Ariyalur to Chidambaram via Jayankondam and Kattumannarkudi will be established, initiating rail service. 2. Fast and special trains will be facilitated to stop at the Ariyalur municipality railway station. Trichy: 1. Trichy Ponmalai railway workshop will be converted into a coach factory, with efforts undertaken in collaboration with BHEL Trichy. 2. A high-level road will be constructed from Palpannai to Thuvakudi on the national highway. Cuddalore: 1. Cashew exporters will be facilitated with the re-provision of a 5% RoTDEP (Remission of Duties and Taxes on Exported Products). 2. The initiative to replace old cashew trees with new high-yielding cashew saplings with subsidy will be undertaken. 3. All types of trains going to southern parts and Chennai will be arranged to stop at Pennadam railway station, improving its standards. Mayiladuthurai: The abandoned Mayiladuthurai to Tarangambadi railway track, closed 30 years ago, will be restored to facilitate travel to Karaikal, Nagore, Velankanni and Thirunallar.

Nagapattinam: Efforts will be made to either convert the Nagai port into a cargo container port or establish a new cargo container port near Vellapallam. Thanjavur: Kamban Express will be re-operated on the Pattukkottai railway route. A new railway line from Pattukkottai to Mannargudi will be established, and a new railway line from Pattukkottai to Thanjavur, a request made by the late leader Kalaignar during DMK's tenure in the Union Government, will be constructed. Thiruvarur: A National Agricultural Research Centre will be established in Thiruvarur, and a law college will be started at Thiruvarur Central University. Pudukkottai: A new railway line connecting Thanjavur, Kandarvakottai, Pudukkottai, Tirupattur, Vellore, and Madurai will be constructed, and the Pudukkottai railway station will be renovated. Dindigul: 1. The feasibility of establishing a rail service between Dindigul and Sabarimala will be explored. 2. A separate train facility from Dindigul to Chennai will be considered. Sivagangai: A four-lane road from Karaikudi to Natham (via Singampunari, Kottampatti) will be constructed. Madurai: 1. DMK will make efforts to reintroduce the Madurai - Tirupati train service. 2. The train service from Madurai to Bodinayakanur will be extended to Lower Camp. 3.

Madurai Airport will be upgraded to international standards, and Union Government industries will be established in Madurai. Theni: 1. The Baby Dam and Mullai Periyar Dam will be strengthened. 2. Value addition factories for bananas and grapes will be established. Virudhunagar: 1. A railway station will be established at Kariapatti. 2. The import of Chinese fireworks will be banned to protect the Sivakasi fireworks industry. Ramanathapuram: 1. A road overbridge will be constructed over the railway line at Pamban to facilitate smooth traffic flow. 2. Tourist ferry/boat services between Rameswaram and Chennai, Rameswaram and Kanyakumari, will be initiated.

Thoothukudi: 1. Efforts will be made to establish ship transportation from Thoothukudi to Sri Lanka. 2. Efforts will be undertaken to upgrade Thoothukudi Airport to an international airport. 3. Additional rail services to Chennai, similar to the Muthunagar Fast Service, will be introduced. 4. A fifth railway gate and overbridge will be constructed in Thoothukudi V.M.S. Nagar. 5. A substitute factory benefiting the public and small traders will be established in the area of the cooperative sugar mill in Thiruchendur, closed 21 years ago, creating job opportunities and protecting the livelihoods of local merchants. 6. New industrial enterprises will be established in the SIDCO industrial estate area in Pidaraneri village near Nazareth,

creating job opportunities for the local population. 7. The railway stations at Kayalpattinam and Meelavittan will be upgraded. 8. Efforts will be made to add value and export palm products produced in Thoothukudi. Tirunelveli: 1. A new railway division will be established with Tirunelveli as the headquarters, incorporating the districts of Tirunelveli, Tenkasi, Thoothukudi and Kanyakumari. 2. A high-level overbridge will be constructed at the railway gate near Rani School on Ambasamudram, V.K.Puram Road. 3. Fishing harbours without breakwaters will be constructed in eight coastal villages in Tirunelveli district.

Tenkasi: 1. The project to connect Tirunelveli and Sankarankovil railway stations through Alangulam, Surandai and Sendamaram, extending to Sankarankovil, will be reactivated. 2. Trains to Kerala, Coimbatore and Chennai will be facilitated to stop at Kadayanallur railway station. Kanyakumari: 1. Immediate compensation will be provided to those who lost their lands for the four-lane road project from Kanyakumari to Kaliyakkavilai and from Kavalkinaru to Nagercoil. 2. A modern fish processing unit will be established in Kanyakumari district to package the fish for export to foreign countries with advanced technology. 3. A rubber factory will be established in the Vilavancode constituency. 4. The AVM canal will be cleaned and made available for public use. In this election manifesto, we pledge to implement all the plans outlined, which will contribute to the progress of Tamil Nadu and the development of India. Candidates contesting under the DMK alliance as members of Parliament will continuously strive to fulfil these promises to the people.

The AIADMK's anti-people and anti-Tamil actions: During its ten-year rule in Tamil Nadu, the AIADMK led the state to the bottom in industrial development in India. Contrary to the Tamil language and ethnic sentiments, it supported the BJP's "One Nation, One X" (Language, Election, Education Policy, etc.) slogans, acting against the overall development of Tamil Nadu. Similarly, by voting against the amendment proposed by the DMK to include Sri Lankan Tamils in the Citizenship Amendment Act, and by providing its crucial votes for the passage of the current version of the CAA, it showed its antipathy to both Sri Lankan Refugees, and Secularism in India. Tamil Nadu and its people know well the record of achievements when the DMK was in the Union Government and even when it ruled the state. We assure that the trust and expectations placed in the DMK will be met without fail, and we will work diligently to ensure the progress of Tamil Nadu and India through this election manifesto.

Therefore, let the fascist rule be banished! We are ready to work tirelessly to maintain India's secular nature. Command us, and we will establish a secular alliance government in India! We will foster harmony among religions! We will protect human rights! We will champion social justice and equality! We will enhance industrial prosperity! We will achieve global acclaim in economic development! We will work incessantly for Democracy to flourish! Let us unite and succeed! Long live Tamil Nadu! Victory to India!

समाजवादी पार्टी भारत में ग़गीबी और जातिवाद एक-दूसरे के पर्यायवाची हैं, एक के साथ दूसरे का उत्थान जुड़ा हुआ है। - नेताजी मुलायम सिंह यादव एक सोच, एक विचारधारा की पहल। पिछड़े, दलित, अल्पसंख्यक एवं आधी आबादी को अवसरों की समानता और भागीदारी दिलाने का जन-आंदोलन। समाज के सभी ग़रीब-कमज़ोर वर्गों का समावेशी विकास कराने का संकल्प। ग़रीबी के ख़िलाफ़ लड़ाई एक धोखा है, जब तक जाति और लिंग के आधार पर भेदभाव के ख़िलाफ़ लड़ाई न हो। - डॉ. राममनोहर लोहिया

हर समाजवादी पार्टी बख्शीश की राजनीति नहीं करती है, समाजवादी पार्टी परिवर्तन की राजनीति करती है। - नेताजी मुलायम सिंह यादव समाजवादी पार्टी की भारत के संविधान में सच्ची एवं डॉ. अम्बेडकर के आदर्शों से प्रेरणा लेकर समाजवादी पार्टी लोकतंत्र, धर्म-निरपेक्षता और समाजवाद में आस्था रखती है। समाजवादी पार्टी का विश्वास ऐसी राजव्यवस्था द में है, जिसमे आर्थिक एवं राजनीतिक सत्ता का विकेन्द्रीकरण निश्चित रूप से हो। ३ हि पिछड़े दलितों, अल्पसंख्यकों एवं महिलाओं के क्त्रा श्ध्कू लिए सामाजिक न्याय पर आधारित विश अवसर के सिद्धांत में समाजवादी पार्टी का ७ ८ द -े विश्वास है। कं लोकतंत्र का सार यह नहीं है कि बहुमत शासन करेगा, बल्कि यह है कि प्रत्येक नागरिक की बात सुनी जाएगी। - डॉ. राममनोहर लोहिया 5 मीडिया की आजादी का अधिकार लोकतांत्रिक संस्थाओं की स्वतंत्रता व स्वायत्तता का अधिकार ७ न्याय और समानता का अधिकार समाजवाद धन के वितरण के बारे में नहीं, शक्ति के वितरण

सुरक्षित वातावरण में जीने का अधिकार आवारा पशुओं से खेत एवं जान को बचाने का अधिकार जातिवादी टिप्पणी से मुक्ति का अधिकार गड्ढे मुक्त सड़कों पर चलने का अधिकार 24 घंटे बिजली की आपूर्ति पाने का अधिकार गरीब को भी उच्च श्रेणी की शिक्षा पाने का अधिकार बेहतर स्वास्थ्य सेवा पाने का अधिकार बेहतर सार्वजनिक परिवहन पाने का अधिकार खेल प्रतिभाओं को निखारने के लिए आवश्यक संसाधन पाने का अधिकार जब तक आप सामाजिक स्वतंत्रता प्राप्त नहीं कर लेते, कानून द्वारा प्रदान की गई कोई भी स्वतंत्रता आपके लिए किसी काम

सामाजिक न्याय > 2025 तक जाति आधारित जनगणना कराएँगे। इसके आधार पर 2029 तक सबको न्याय एवं हिस्सेदारी सुनिश्चित करेंगे। > 2025 तक अनुसूचित जाति, अनुसूचित जनजाति एवं पिछड़े वर्ग के सभी सरकारी रिक्त पदों को भरेंगे। > निजी क्षेत्र में समाज के सभी वर्गों की भागीदारी सुनिश्चित करेंगे। > 2029 तक भूख से मुक्ति। > 2029 तक गरीबी का पूर्ण उन्मूलन। देश की समृद्धि का रास्ता गांवों के खेतों एवं खलिहानों से होकर गुजरता है। “चौधरी चरण सिंह ॥

किसान कल्याण > दुग्ध सहित सभी फसलों के लिए एमएसपी। एमएसपी की गणना स्वामीनाथन फॉर्मूले (सी 2+50%) के आधार पर की जाएगी। » कानूनी गारंटी के रूप में सभी किसानों को एमएसपी। > भूमिहीन किसानों समेत सभी कृषि/किसान ऋण 2024 में माफ किए जाएंगे। » किसानों की सिचाई मुफ़्त की जाएगी। > कृषि ऋण की निगरानी करने और किसानों को नियमित आधार पर राहत प्रदान करने के लिए किसान आयोग का गठन किया जाएगा। > भूमिहीन/किरायेदार किसानों सहित सभी छोटे और सीमांत किसानों (2.5 एकड़ से कम भूमि) के लिए 5000 रुपये प्रति माह की पेंशन दी जाएगी। > सभी कृषि प्रधान राज्यों में हर 70 किलोमीटर पर एक मंडी की स्थापना। » यूपी में गन्ना किसानों के लिए समय पर भुगतान सुनिश्चित करने के लिए 0000 करोड़ रुपये के रोलिंग फंड की स्थापना की जाएगी। » मनरेगा द्वारा निजी कृषि मजदूरों को उनकी मजदूरी का 40% भुगतान किया जाएगा। छोटा लक्ष्य अपराध है, महान लक्ष्य होना चाहिए। - एपीजे अब्दुल कलाम

युवा और रोजगार 2 मनरेगा के तहत मजदूरी बढ़ाकर 450 रुपये की जाएगी और कार्य के दिन (9 63५५) 50 तक किये जाएँगे। >> मनरेगा की तर्ज पर शहरी रोजगार गारंटी अधिनियम 2024 के पहले संसदीय सत्र में लागू किया जाएगा। » सभी रिक्त पड़ी सरकारी नौकरियों को तत्काल भरा जाएगा। > सभी के लिए राष्ट्रीय रोजगार नीति और मिशन रोजगार स्थापित किया जाएगा। > युवाओं के लिए लैपटॉप वितरण योजना पूरे देश में लागू की जाएगी। >> पेपर लीक और प्रतियोगी परीक्षाओं में भ्रष्टाचार को पूर्ण रूप से समाप्त किया जाएगा। पौष्टिक भोजन का अधिकार सबसे मूलभूत अधिकार होता है और किसी भी सरकार की सबसे पहली जिम्मेदारी। - श्री अखिलेश यादव

'आटा-डाटा' का अधिकार > मुफ़्त राशन में गेहूँ की जगह आटा देंगे। पौष्टिकता और गुणवत्ता में ये आटा देश की सबसे अच्छी कंपनियों के आटे के मुकाबले का होगा। इसके लिए मंडियों के पास ही अत्याधुनिक तकनीकी वाला 'आटा प्लांट' लगाएंगे, जिससे स्थानीय स्तर पर लोगों को रोज़गार भी मिलेगा। हर राशनकार्ड धारी परिवार को 500 रूपये का मोबाइल डाटा मुफ़्त देंगे। आज के जीवन में मोबाइल का इस्तेमाल हर ज़रूरी सेवा, सूचना- संचार व शिक्षा के लिए हर एक की ज़रूरत है, चाहे वो शहर हो यागाँव। मुफ़्त डाटा से डिजिटल सम्पन्न बनाम डिजिटल विपन्न में 'डिजिटल डिवाइड' का अंतर नहीं रह जाएगा। गैर-बराबरी मिटाने के समाजवादी मूल्यों की ओर समाजवादी पार्टी की सोच का यह एक प्रगतिशील कदम होगा। रोटी कपड़ा सस्ती हो, दवा पढ़ाई मुफ्ती हो। - डॉ. राममनोहर लोहिया ५ नि ४ »> निश्ुल्क शिक्षा का अधिकार सुनिश्चित करेंगे।

» शिक्षा के लिए बजट जीडीपी के 3३% से दोगुना कर 6% किया जाएगा एवं गुणवत्ता के लिए मिशन चलाया जाएगा। >> 50॥ 895९१ ४०८०४ं०। ३। ६१ए८३०धं०7' पर विशेष ध्यान दिया जाएगा और इंडस्ट्रीज से जोड़ा जाएगा। »> सभी के लिए गुणव्त्तापूर्ण स्वास्थ्य देखभाल के अधिकार की कानून द्वारा गारंटी दी जाएगी। > स्वास्थ्य पर वर्तमान सार्वजनिक व्यय को दोगुना कर जीडीपी के 3.5% तक लाया जाएगा। »> सरकार विद्यार्थियों को न्यूनतम व्याजदर पर शिक्षा-लोन उपलब्ध कराएगी। » डिजिटल डिवाइड को समाप्त करेंगे। शिक्षा का उद्देश्य हमें यह सिखाना होना चाहिए कि कैसे सोचना है, ना कि क्‍या सोचना है। - डॉ. राममनोहर लोहिया समान मानसिक क्षमताओं से युक्त नारी, पुरुष की साथी है। उसे * पुरुष की प्रत्येक गतिविधियों में स्वतंत्रता एवं समानता के साथ भाग लेने का अधिकार है। पुरुष के समान नारी भी अपने क्षेत्र की गतिविधियों की सर्वोच्च भागीदार है। - महात्मा गांधी

महिला सशक्तिकरण > महिलाओं के ख़िलाफ़ अपराध के लिए जीरो टॉलरेंस एवं देशव्यापी हेल्पलाइन। > परिसीमन की प्रतीक्षा किए बिना 2 साल के भीतर संसद एवं विधानसभाओं में महिलाओं के लिए ३३% आरक्षण सुनिश्चित किया जाएगा, जिसके भीतर दलित, पिछड़े एवं अल्पसंख्यक वर्ग की महिलाओं को उनकी संख्या के अनुपात में भागीदारी दी जाएगी। »>" पुलिस सहित सरकारी नौकरियों में महिलाओं के लिए 3३% आरक्षण। > 'फ्री गर्ल चाइल्ड एजुकेशन' कन्याओं के लिए 'केजी से पीजी तक मुफ्त शिक्षा" »> गरीबी रेखा से नीचे की महिलाओं को 3000 रुपये प्रति माह तक की मासिक पेंशन दी जाएगी। जाति व्यवस्था केवल श्रम का विभाजन नहीं है। यह मजदूरों का भी एक विभाजन है। - डॉ. अम्बेडकर

श्रम कल्याण > न्यूनतम दैनिक मजदूरी को बढ़ाकर 450 रुपये किया जाएगा। > केंद्रीय व राज्य सार्वजनिक क्षेत्र की इकाइयों के निजीकरण को बंद किया जाएगा एवं छंटनी रोकी जाएगी। > सभी असंगठित क्षेत्र के श्रमिकों के लिए 0 लाख का बीमा और 5000 प्रति माह पेंशन की व्यवस्था की जाएगा । > पुरानी पेंशन योजना (079) को पैरा-मिलिट्री सहित सभी के लिए फिर से बहाल किया जाएगा। > असंगठित क्षेत्र के स्किल-अनस्किल श्रमिकों को 500 रुपये प्रति माह 'अमिक सम्मान निधि! प्रदान किया जाएगा।

जंगल और पर्यावरण > जलवायु परिवर्तन और प्रदूषण नियंत्रण पर राष्ट्रीय चार्टर और नीति बनायी जाएगी। > 2029 तक निर्माण उद्योग के लिए प्राकृतिक संसाधनों के सभी खनन को चरणबद्ध तरीके से समाप्त कर दिया जाएगा और पर्यावरण के अनुकूल टेक्नोलॉजी को प्रोत्साहित किया जाएगा। > गंगा एवं यमुना नदियों को प्रदुषण मुक्त करते हुए अविरल करने के प्रयास किये जायेंगे |

| उद्योगऔर वाणिज्य] > एमएसएमई के सूक्ष्म क्षेत्र क विकास पर विशेष ध्यान दिया जाएगा और बुनकर समुदाय के लिए विशेष योजनायें बनाई जाएगी। > अनुसंधान एवं विकास पर बजट दोगुना किया जाएगा। > भारत को &। (#॥धाटां३ ॥5६2९॥ 8९॥८९) में अग्रणी बनाएँगे। > निर्यात में वृद्धि कर वैश्विक व्यापार में हिस्सेदारी को दोगुना करने का लक्ष्य > कुटीर उद्योगों को पुनर्जीवित किया जायेगा । > 69» 7 प्रणाली के विपरीत प्रभावों का मूल्यांकन करते हुए उसमें बुनियादी सुधार लाएंगे। शांति केवल युद्ध की अनुपस्थिति नहीं है, बल्कि न्याय, कानून और व्यवस्था की उपस्थिति है। - डॉ. राममनोहर लोहिया

रक्षा और विदेश नीति > अग्निवीर नीति को समाप्त किया जाएगा। सशस्त्र बलों में नियमित भर्ती एक बार फिर से शुरू की जाएगी। > भारत में रक्षा उपकरणों के निर्माण पर जोर दिया जाएगा, रक्षा क्षेत्र का स्वदेशीकरण होगा। रक्षा क्षेत्र को पर्याप्त बजट दिया जाएगा और इस क्षेत्र का आधुनिकीकरण किया जाएगा। राष्ट्र की 5९५ और 5०0५४९/९४॥४५ की हर स्थिति में रक्षा की जाएगी। > विशेष रूप से चीन और पाकिस्तान के संदर्भ में हम अपनी सीमाओं की रक्षा करेंगे। घुसपैठ या आतंकवाद को बढ़ावा देना बर्दाश्त नहीं किया जाएगा।

ग्रामीण विकास > 'स्मार्ट विलेज क्लस्टर' पूरे देश में स्थापित किए जाएंगे, जो 'स्मार्ट विलेज क्लस्टर डेवलपमेंट अथॉरिटी' के माध्यम से कार्यान्वित किए जाएंगे। > ये क्लस्टर बड़े पैमाने पर डेटा मैपिंग और &। (४।धा८ा३। ॥0९॥8०॥८९) द्वारा अनुमानों पर आधारित होंगे। प्रत्येक क्लस्टर में स्थानीय और सस्टेनेबल टेक्नोलॉजी आधारित निम्नलिखित समाधान शामिल हैं। ७ इंफ्रास्ट्रक्चर- सड़क, वेस्ट मैनैजमेंट, वैकल्पिक विद्युत ग्रिड, ग्रीन टेक्नोलॉली सहित सस्ते आवास और जल संरक्षण। > शिक्षा और स्वास्थ्य देखभाल। > कृषि। > क्षमता निर्माण, कौशल विकास और स्थानीय रोजगार। ७) सूक्ष्म उद्यम। < ग्रामीण स्तर पर ई-गवर्नेंस के माध्यम से जनसुविधा केंद्रों को इंसेटिवाइज किया जाएगा। >> लोहिया आवास योजना, जनेश्वर मिश्र ग्राम योजना को स्मार्ट विलेज प्रोजेक्ट से जोड़कर शुरू किया जाएगा। अर्थव्यवस्था > सार्वभौमिक विकास के लिए लक्षित नीतियां बनायीं जाएंगी ताकि प्रति व्यक्ति आय (9९7 ८००५० ॥८07॥6 ) में सुधार किया जा सके। शहरी विकास > अत्याधुनिक सुविधाओं वाले और विकास के इंजन के रूप में संरचित आधुनिक शहरों का निर्माण किया जाएगा।

पर्यटन > भारत को एक बहु-अनुभव विश्व स्तरीय पर्यटन स्थल के रूप में प्रदर्शित करने के लिए ५४००० ८०५५ ॥#मम८पलपा९ विकसित किया जायेगा एवं वैश्विक अभियान चलाकर रोड शो किये जाएँगे। 2027 तक 2 करोड़ अंतर्राष्ट्रीय पर्यटक आगमन का लक्ष्य रखा जाएगा। > देशीय पर्यटन में विशेष रूप से ध्यान दिया जायेगा परिवहन > रेलवे समेत सार्वजनिक परिवहन में वरिष्ठ नागरिकों को मिलने वाली छूट को फिर से लागू करेंगे।

JUSTICE, social,economic and political; LIBERTY of thought, expression, belief, faith and worship ; EQUALITY of status and of opportunity; FRATERNITY assuring the dignity of the individual | and the unity of the Nation; IN OUR CONSTITUENT ASSEMBLY his twenty- ENACT AND GIVE TO OURSELVES THIS CONSTITUTION. Preface Dear Voters, under the able leadership of Ajitdada Pawar, the Nationalist Congress Party, as an ally of NDA, is presenting its Manifesto with the slogan “New hope, New Direction’. Our country is the world’s largest democracy with rich diversity. Each Indian citizen has the duty and right to vote for who represents and leads our country. India has the potential to lead the world. In the last decade, under the leadership of Prime Minister Narendra Modiji India has ascended a pinnacle of glory among all countries.

The Nationalist Congress Party is contesting the Lok Sabha elections under leadership of Ajitdada Pawar with the motto ‘Nationalists for the Nation’. The constitution bestowed upon us by Bharat Ratna Dr. Babasahab Ambedkar has allowed us to set aside the differences of casteism. This is our culture. The Nationalist Congress Party is known as the party that strictly adheres to its principles. Born in the land of Maharashtra Yugapurush Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj pioneered the mantra of people’s Swarajya and good governance. Great Saints like Saint Dnyaneshwar, Saint Tukaram, Saint Eknath, Sant Janabai, Sant Muktabai, Saint Goroba Kaka, Saint Chokhoba, Saint Sawata Maharaj, Saint Basweshwara Swami, Saint Jagnade Maharaj, Saint Jumdev Baba inculcated the mantra of humanitarian approach to all. Maharashtra is the birth place of many great human beings like the pioneer of Swarajyarakshak Sambhaji Maharaj, Mahatma Jyotiba Phule, Shahu Maharaj, Dr.

Babasaheb Ambedkar, Maulana Azad, Saint Gadge Baba, Saint Tukdoji Maharaj, Annabhau Satke, Birsa Munda, Shahir Amar Shaikh who were all visionaries and social reformers of India. Rashtramata Rajmata Jijau Masaheb, Punyashlok Ahilyadevi Holkar, Kranti Jyoti Savitridevi Phule, Mata Ramai made invaluable contributions in building Maharashtra. Nationalist Congress Party is committed to Women Empowerment as inspired by the philosophy and morals of these great women. The essence and legacy of their ideas is the core of the Nationalist Congress Party. Equality, fraternity, and all-inclusiveness are the breath and soul of NCP.

NCP believes in “Sabka Saath, Sabka Vikas, Sabka Prayas, Sabka Vishwas’. Today’s Lok Sabha election is important for the trinity of “Nation’s Development, Nation’s Trust and Nation’s support”. This Lok Sabha election is crucial so that every citizen of India will feel proud to be a citizen of developed India. Stability is the foundation for a family, and it is the duty of every voter to choose a stable government. The stable government speeds up the decision-making process in the interest of the nation. Large projects and welfare schemes can be implemented with consensus. You must participate in this Lok Sabha election as a well-informed voter with a futuristic vision. You must vote for the nation’s stability. NCP is committed to strengthen the leadership of Prime Minister Narendra Modiji in the role of “Nationalists for the Nation”.

Having faith in the leadership of Prime Minister Narendra Modiji, Nationalist Congress Party leader Ajitdada Pawar has joined hands with BJP led NDA in Mahayuti. As a finance minister of the state, while implementing fiscal discipline in Maharashtra, it is being ensured that citizens get best services and facilities by balancing the revenue collection and expenditure. The NCP is trying to keep the state ahead in health, education and other sectors. Maharashtra has the largest number of foreign investors in the country due to its investor friendly policies. The Nationalist Congress Party has been continuously implementing various schemes and programmes for the upliftment and progress of Scheduled Castes, Scheduled Tribes, Adivasis and other backward classes by bringing them into mainstream for social justice.

Deputy Chief Minister Ajitdada Pawar has taken ambitious decisions for safety of minority community and their inclusion in the mainstream. The share capital of Maulana Azad Corporation has been increased to Rs. 500 crore and substantial increase is given in the scholarships for higher studies in foreign countries. To ensure no community in the state is left behind, planning is made to provide financial assistance by establishing independent corporations.

The Nationalist Congress Party has always been known as a progressive and reformist party. The NCP believes in the trinity of ‘Truth, Non-violence and Love’ a concept of the Father of the nation, Mahatma Gandhi. The architect of united Maharashtra, Hon’ble Yashwantrao Chavan Saheb is the inspiration of NCP. Our party respects and is proud of the modern and progressive thoughts of Yashwantrao Chavan Saheb. His cultured politics and progressive ideas are the legacy of the NCP. With inspiration from Shiv-Phule-Shahu- Ambedkar may the fraternity in the country increase day by day.

The Nationalist Congress Party is soliciting votes to make India a Vishwa Guru under the leadership of Prime Minister Narendra Modiji. We are confident that the public will continue to repose faith in Prime Minister Narendra Modiji. The NCP is contesting the polls under the able leadership of Ajitdada Pawar with the “Clock symbol”. We appeal to you to press the button in front of the clock symbol and make the NCP candidates win with a huge majority. A vote to Nationalist Congress Party means a vote for Nation’s progress. Brothers and sisters, exercise your vote and elect worthy representatives. Vote for the candidates of the Nationalist Congress Party contesting on Clock symbol and the candidates of the alliance parties — Mahayuti, to safeguard the destiny of Maharashtra for the next five years. Jai Hind, Jai Maharashtra, Jai Rashtravadi! Sunil Tatkare President - Maharashtra

Our Stand With the target of five trillion dollar econmy, India is on its way to become the third- largest economy in the world and a developed nation. This will be possible due to all inclusive development policies of Union Government under the leadership of Prime Minister Narendra Modiji. India had occupied an important place at global level. In the crisis situations like war, economic crisis or social disruption, India has emerged as a dominant force in the true sense. Prime Minister Narendra Modiji's stature as a Vishwa Guru for India has been proved. Under his leadership, our nation is heading for 'Viksit Bharat' in 2047 from the present Amrit Kal. The Indian economy has evolved as the top five economies of the world by promoting all-round development in every sector and with the objective of development in all sections of society.

Many countries have responded well to the digital infrastructure developed by India as an effective measure to empower the poor and to reduce their dependence on the cash economy. Under the able leadership of Prime Minister Narendra Modiiji, all states in the country are experiencing balanced development and the GDP of the nation has also increased. In both 2014 and 2019 elections, the NDAgot the absolute majority. Still, only after the verdict of the Supreme Court, Prime Minister Narendra Modiji proceeded to build the grand temple of Lord Shri Ram. The Ram Temple issue was thus settled. This was welcomed by all sections of the society. Prime Minister Narendra Modiji has led our country in the fight against the Corona virus Pandemic with transparency and skillful administration. Ambitious decisions and far reaching projects are implemented in the States and Union Territories like Lakshadweep, Jammu and Kashmir,

Andaman and Nicobar, Ladakh, along with the North Eastern states which were earlier neglected in the development journey of the nation. Under Prime Minister Modiji's Leadership, India's diplomacy in dealing with both Pakistan and China has proved its independent and autonomous foreign policy at the international level. Also, the establishment of cordial relations of unprecedented friendship with Islamic countries has led to the construction of a grand Hindu temple there too. This is a matter of pride for our Indian culture and our foreign policy.

Prime Minister Narendra Modiji and India were showered with praise at the G20 summit for various achievements. In the last 10 years, the NDA government under the leadership of Prime Minister Narendra Modiji has successfully implemented all- inclusive schemes of public benefits along with development projects. These include Jandhan Yojana, Skill Development Mission, Make in India, Mission Swachh Bharat, Shram-Eva-Jayate Yojana, Beti Bachao Beti Padhao Yojana, Prime Minister Mudra Yojana, Smart City, Amrut Mission, Ujjwala Yojana, Prime Minister Suraksha Bima Yojana, Pension scheme, Digital India Start-up India, Namami Gange Yojana, Ayushman Bharat, Jaljeevan Mission, Kisan Sanman Nidhi, Vishwakarma Yojana and Pradhan Mantri Nari Shakti Yojana. Due to the benefits of these schemes, a strong foundation has been laid for citizens to be Atma Nirbhar. Nationalist Congress Party believes that today's Atma Nirbhar Bharat will be 'Viksit Bharat' in the year 2047, the centenary year of independence.

Following this path of national development, the government of Maharashtra has also undertaken balanced development plans. Vidarbha, Marathwada, Konkan, the rest of Maharashtra, and the capital city of Mumbai are all playing important roles in increasing the state's Human Development Index due to the creation of a wide network of infrastructure projects and promoting, logistics, trade, and industry. Our resolve is Maharashtra a “Partner in Nation’s Development” and the “Nationalists for the Nation” is our vision.

Maharashtra Maharashtra is known as the financial capital of the country which promotes financial inclusiveness. Apart from this, Maharashtra also tops with favourable scores in the social and economic indicators set by the Centre. Maharashtra is also known as the most progressive state in the country with its leadership in economic and industrial sectors. Maharashtra has resolved to regain its past glory of economic progress by making every effort to be a 1 trillion dollar economy. We believe that in the future, this resolution will play a decisive role in fulfilling the aspirations of all sections of society and youth, in particular by boosting employment generation.

The Mahayuti Government is implementing infrastructure projects worth Rs. 8 Lakh Crore across the state. Assets will be created if the infrastructure is developed. Asset creation will generate employment. This will increase revenue & the revenue increase will give raise to purchasing power. The government firmly believes that the flow of money in the economy will develop the economy. The priority of the Mahayuti government is to take the size of the economy of the Mumbai Metropolitan Region to $300 billion by 2030 and complete various infrastructure projects in the region. The government is also promoting agriculture and agro-based industries with emphasis on manufacturing and engineering services.

The Nationalist Congress Party, which preserves the legacy of progressive ideas of Shiv- Phule-Shahu- Ambedkar, has consistently taken the role of inclusive development while maintaining social harmony. The Nationalist Congress Party is following the same Path. NCP has given priority to women's empowerment and gender equality. Along with its allies, the NCP recently announced the state's fourth women's policy on the occasion of International Women's Day. The policy aims to increase the Participation of women in all spheres of society. The NCP is committed to remain the preferred and trusted party of the people in the state with excellent service and development. As our nation has completed 75 years of independence, we are handing over this manifesto of the Nationalist Congress Party to the people for the Lok Sabha 2024 election. In this Amrit Kal, we are confident that the people will accept the role of "Nationalists for the Nation". Ajit Pawar National President

Nationalist Congress is supporting and participating in Sabka Saath - Sabka Vikas campaign It includes, Free Ration distribution to 80 crore citizens of India. The scheme will be extended for next 5 years. 50 crore beneficiaries in Jan Dhan Yojana Pukka Houses allotted to 4 Core Citizens More than 25 crore citizens have been brought over the poverty-line. More than 46 crore citizens have benefited by more than Rs. 27 Lakh Crore loans from Mudra Yojana. Constitutional status given to National Backward Class Commission. Loans sanctioned to more than 63 lakh street vendors under Prime Minister Swanidhi Yojana Free power supply through Prime Minister Surya Ghar Yojana. Agriculture The farmers are the backbone of India's progress, ascent, and prosperity. Strengthening the farmers will strengthen the nation's prosperity. Nationalist Congress Party is the party that respects farmers and agriculture. The farmers of Maharashtra have always been an important component of the progress of our nation. Therefore, our party is committed to strengthen the farmers and that is our resolution. Self-sufficient farmers, prosperous farmers is our objective.

Minimum Support Price (MSP) is the right of farmers, which we will protect. We ensure that the farm produce will be protected from damage caused by nature’s fury by establishing necessary logistic facilities alongwith markets. Also we will ensure financial aid to such farmers. We will promote technology and collective farming to increase farm productivity. We will create a chain of Farmers Producer Organisations (FPOs) to enable the farmers to be exporters themselves. NREGA will be linked to Agriculture sector to provide skilled labour and to reduce cost of production. We will strengthen the horticulture, floriculture, fisheries, sericulture, poultry, sheep and goat rearing and agriculture allied industries. We will promote organic farming for healthy diets and strong citizens. We will implement all-inclusive farmers centric policy for ethanol production and export for sugarcane growers.

We are committed to ensure that the ethanol producing industries do not suffer due to fluctuations in the global market and the interests of the farmers are safeguarded. We will strengthen the solar, wind and hydro-electric power projects to generate non-conventional energy for sustainable power supply to increase agricultural productivity and income and to reduce the dependence of farmers on conventional power supply. We will make the crop insurance scheme more accessible by expanding the scope of Agricultural crop Insurance Scheme. We will urge the Central Government to unify the other coastal states for granting fisheries a status of Fish Farming. Farm fencing scheme will be implemented to protect the farms from wild animals and other elements. Currently, each farmer receives Rs. 6 thousand per year from the Central Government under the ‘Pradhan Mantri Kisan Sanman Nidhi’ Scheme. The State Government also contributes Rs. 6 Thousand to make the total Rs. 12 thousand. We will make all efforts to raise this amount substantially.

Employment / Industry India is leaping ahead towards becoming a $ 5 trillion economy in which Maharashtra will contribute a lion's share of $ 1 trillion economy. The path is set to fulfil the resolution of 'Viksit Bharat’. Nationalist Congress Party is committed to ensure that Maharashtra remains the main contributor of progress of developed India. Maharashtra is the number one state in the country in collection of Goods and Service Tax. The share of GST in state's own revenue will increase further to make Maharashtra's contribution the highest in the economic progress of the nation. Maharashtra is the largest industrialised state in the country. Maharashtra accounts for 14.2% of the country's GDP. Maharashtra accounts for 17.3% of total exports. So, we will ensure that the leap will be even higher as there is a Mahayuti Government in the state and NDA led Governmentin the centre. Maharashtra is the leading state in the country to attract Foreign direct investment. Maharashtra alone accounts for 38.78% of the total FDI in the country. At the World Economic Forum (WEF) in Davos in January 2024, the state signed MoUs with more than Rs.

3 Lakh 53 thousand Crore with various international companies. With these MoUs, 2 Lakh jobs will be generated in the state. It is our resolution to provide “work for every hand and income to every family” by promoting domestic and foreign investments in the state. We will ensure sustainable credit supply and market access to industries by establishing and promoting Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises by combining textile enabling technologies with Khadi and Village Industries. We will place emphasis on the policies that will ensure economic self-reliance and long-term sustainability for large scale employment creating enterprises such as handlooms and machine looms. At present loans of up to Rs. 10 Lakh are issued from Mudra Yojana. We will try to increase the loan amount to Rs.20 Lakh.

Human Resources Everyone has different skills and abilities. Making each person economically self-reliant and industry friendly helps local employment and decentralization of industries. We believe that new entrepreneurs will come up in rural areas by effectively implementing the policies of skill development & thus paving the way for decentralisation of industries. This will generate large employment opportunities if combined with Human Resources development. We are committed to make Maharashtra the skill capital of the nation. By expanding the scope of Vishwakarma Scheme, efforts will be made to expand the industries based on modern technology of skill development for 12 Balutedar classes. The Nationalist Congress Party will strive to increase the remuneration of skill trained youth working on contract basis in private companies up to Rs. 20 thousand per month. “Atma Nirbhar Bharat is our resolution”. Women and Child Welfare, Gender The Nationalist Congress Party is committed to provide opportunities to women, girls, and children of the state to implement the right to equality given to all citizens by the constitution of India and implement its welfare schemes and policies for their development in social, economic and other spheres. Matrushakti and Matrusanman are the thread of our party.

Maharashtra State was announced on Women's Day, 8th March, 2024, with specific and qualitative indicators for women empowerment and as an index to measure progress in the implementation. To respect motherhood in the traditional paternal system, it has been made mandatory for everyone to put the name of their own mother before the father's name. We are determined to implement this revolutionary decision of giving respect to motherhood at the national level. The NCP was always insistent for 33 percent reservation for women. In accordance with it the Union Government has enacted a legislation providing 33 percent reservation for Assemblies. We will emphasize on making available of safe market and financial facilities for women self-help groups and women entrepreneurs. We will continue to insist that there should be a stern policy at the national level to prevent Human Trafficking for the safety of women.

We will try to increase scope and spread of 'Lek Ladki’ scheme for the dignity of a girl child. Social and political empowerment of women will be emphasized for equal opportunities for them in the political sphere. We urge support for child safety and violence free childhood and provision of increased manpower and resources to curb sexual offences against children with the strict enforcement of POCSO. Special attention will be given to ensure for adequate provision in the budget for child welfare and specially abled children as well as for all children who need care and safety across the nation. Working Women's Hostels will be constructed for the working women in the cities.

Youth and Sports The youth is the foundation and future of our country. Employment and motivation for ideas is the path for successful bright future. The Nationalist Congress Party is known as a Youth oriented and youth centric party. We will focus on culture, respect and morals for their happiness and prosperity. We will emphasize on increasing the contribution of the youth power in the progress of the nation. We will ensure equal access to education for the youth. We promise to provide employment to our youth. We will emphasize that substantial provision will be made for sports and the playgrounds which will ensure that international standard training centres will be set up for our sports persons. The players who bring glory to the nation on an international level will be suitably honoured. We will continue to appeal for greater encouragement and opportunities for young athletes across the nation under ‘Khelo India” campaign. Preference must be given to transparent recruitments. We will insist that stern action is taken against the paper leaks.

Senior Citizens About 10% of the population of India is above the age of 60. It is expected to reach at 12% by 2030 and 20% by 2050. Therefore, it will be our priority to implement various measures for the dignity and safety of senior citizens. This age group is called as ‘Silver Economy'. We believe that initiatives will be taken for participation of this economy to the mainstream economy. Our party will insist that the people above 60 years of age get 50% concession in the rail and metro travel across the country. We will appeal for Ayushyaman Bharat Yojana_ to be applicable to all citizens over the age of 70 years. Social Justice / Minorities / Other

Backward Classes Our party believes in the right to live as a human being irrespective of caste, creed and religion. The party believes in equality, fraternity and unity. The NCP has faith in Sane Guruji's quote “The true religion is to offer love to the world”. It is our duty to bring the deprived and backward sections of the society into the mainstream of the society. We will demand for a caste based census. We will endeavour to have Maulana Azad Research and Training Institute at the national level. We will continue to push for the implementation of measures and funds to ensure that the students from minority communities will get opportunities of higher education, entrepreneurship and employment through this institution. We will try to upgrade the educational quality of the Urdu Medium Schools to Semi English Medium Schools in order to raise the educational standards of the Urdu Medium Schools.

We will insist on the service and facility centric policy for Haj Pilgrims. Our priority will be to ensure education and employment opportunities for girls in the minority sect. We will try for the special financial policy by the centre. We will focus on safety measures for citizens travelling abroad for jobs and employment, especially in the Gulf counties. It will be our endeavour to establish a special education / training mechanism for the youth belonging to the scheduled castes and scheduled tribes to set up enterprises. We will try to have a policy for opening engineering and medical colleges for educational institutes belonging to scheduled castes and scheduled tribes. ‘Our party will try to set up an independent ministry for implementation of OBC welfare schemes at the national level on the lines of Maharashtra.

Urban Development Cities are considered to be the engines of growth. Safe, pleasant and eco-friendly cities are considered to be assets of the country. Maharashtra's urbanisation rate is 46%. That means, half of Maharashtra is urban and hence urban development is one of the leading priorities of the NCP. Our policy is to accelerate development projects which enhance the livelihood of the citizen by enabling urban infrastructure and transport facilities. Our party ensures affordable housing, network of public transport, a safe city for women and a dignified life for senior citizens. Our party gives importance to environment, aesthetics, art and culture, gardens and playgrounds for a clean city and pollution free air. Our party will ensure that the indigenous tribal citizens of newly created Nagar Panchayats and Municipal Councils in the tribal dominated areas will not be deprived of the benefits of tribal welfare measures. We will insist that 3 crore new dwellings under PM Awas Yojana should be available.

Rural Development A village is an integral unit of the country's development. The village is also the centre that conserves the Indian tradition and culture. NCP is committed for strengthening and decentralization of Panchayati Raj System of rural development. We will focus on building eco-friendly and self-reliant villages. Health-Education-Sanitation- Environment and Employment are on party's five-point agenda to improve the rural areas. NCP will play an active role in the new scheme of Pradhan Mantri Shet- Shivar-Panand Rasta on the Lines of Pradhan Mantri Gram Sadak Yojana. Public Health We will ensure provision of affordable and qualitative health service on the lines of AIMS. For modernization of Primary Health centres, we will make available the best of medical services to the people in rural areas. We will try our best to ensure free medical treatment up to Rs. 5 Lakh to poor and needy persons under Ayushyaman Bharat Yojana

Water Supply Water conservation and water supply is the need of the future. Our party is committed to accord highest priority for the necessity of Water Policy at the national level for equal distribution and sharing of water. We insist that at the national level, river linking projects, river cleaning projects should be taken up at a large scale. Our party will ensure the projects with ambitious and giving far-reaching positive impacts such as Krishna- Bheema stabilisation project, Nar-Par scheme and the diversion of water from the Western Ghats to the drought- affected areas should be declared as national projects.

Climate Change and Global warming are challenges. As a solution, our party proposes to implement the Maharashtra State Climate Change Action Plan with public participation. Efforts will be made for the provision of technology and financial assistance from the Union Government. A policy will be formulated to find solutions on climate change by preparing a special programme for pollution-free rivers. ea Provision of 9.35% of the funds is made for 9.35% tribal population in the state under the budget. The NCP's stand will be that there should be an independent reserved fund for the tribal community at the national level in proportion to the tribal population on the lines of Maharashtra.

Tribal welfare The precipitation is high in forest areas. However, according to the Forest Act, building a water body is prohibited in the forests. As a result, water scarcity is caused in the forests due to hot summers and droughts. So, the NCP will pursue to amend the law to create water reserves in the forest areas and we will appeal for the “Birsa Munda Water Conservation Scheme” to be implemented at the national level. Classical Language status to Marathi

Maharashtra's mother tongue Marathi has a glorious history. Sant Dnyaneshwar has taken Marathi language to the next Level by writing a humanistic book 'Dnyaneshwari' in the Twelfth Century. This is a classic treasure of Marathi language and culture. We are very proud of that. The NCP is constantly trying to get Marathi, the classical Language status and this is the right of Marathi conscious and Marathi people. Poet Suresh Bhat captures the mood of Marathi people and conveys importance of Marathi language as: "We are fortunate that we speak Marathi. We are happy that we listen to Marathi" Maharashtra’'s Architect should be honoured

with Bharat Ratna Award. The architect of 'United Maharashtra’, Hon'ble Yashwantrao Chavan Saheb is the inspiration of NCP. His political life was cultured and respected. As the nation's Deputy Prime Minister, Defence Minister, Chairman of the Eighth Finance Commission and the first Chief Minister of United Maharashtra, he has a lion's share in building the nation and Maharashtra. Hon'ble Yashwantrao Chavan Saheb has contributed to form the united Maharashtra state and glorifying it on India's map. NCP will play an important role in ensuring that Hon'ble Yashwantrao Chavan Saheb is honored with the Bharat Ratna.

Foreign Policy India is the nation that has given the world Buddha's message of peace. ‘Vasudhaiva Kutumbakam' is our soul. Humanism and fraternity will flourish in the world. This is NCP stand. India has taken a strong stand against global terrorism. Despite the scourge of terrorism, India has always been successful in maintaining friendly relations with its neighbours. We assure that NCP will continue to support the foreign policy of the Union government. NCP wishes that India should get a permanent seat in the UN Security Council. The Election Commission of India has allotted Clock symbol to Ajit Anantrao Pawar led Nationalist Congress Party (NCP). The matter is presently subjudice before the Hon'ble Supreme Court and subject to final verdict, the Hon'ble Supreme Court in the interim has allowed the NCP to use the 'Clock' symbol for all ensuing election campaigns.
